seoTitle: Campbelltown vs Campsie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Campbelltown and Campsie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Campbelltown and Campsie.
heading: Campbelltown vs Campsie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Campbelltown, New South Wales and Campsie, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Campsie has a lower overall climate risk score (45/100) compared to Campbelltown (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Campbelltown or Campsie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Campbelltown has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Campsie scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Campbelltown scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Campsie scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Campbelltown or Campsie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Campsie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
